name: Birthdays
description: Cake, candles, and customs — birthday traditions from around the world.

questions.q: What is the name of the most-famous birthday song, copyrighted for decades by Warner/Chappell?

source.notes: Adapted from 'Good Morning to All' (1893). Public domain since 2016 in U.S.

questions.q: What is the name of the 15th birthday celebration in Hispanic cultures, marking a girl's coming-of-age?

source.notes: From Spanish quince (15) + años (years).

questions.q: What is the Filipino name for a girl's lavish 18th birthday celebration?

source.notes: Often features '18 candles' and '18 roses' rituals.

questions.q: What is the Korean 1st-birthday celebration where a baby chooses an item to predict their future?

source.notes: The 'doljabi' ritual offers items like a pen, money, or thread for fortune-telling.

questions.q: Which country famously sends a card from the monarch to citizens turning 100?

source.original_a: 100th and 105th birthdays and every year thereafter
source.notes: Then again at 105 and every year after.

questions.q: What candy-filled object, often shaped like an animal, do kids smash at parties in Mexican tradition?

source.notes: Originally Aztec; adopted into Christmas posadas before becoming a birthday staple.

questions.q: Which long noodles are eaten in many Asian cultures (especially Chinese) for birthdays to symbolize long life?

source.notes: Mandarin: chángshòu miàn (长寿面). Eaten without breaking the strand.

questions.q: Which milestone Chinese birthday celebrates the completion of a full zodiac cycle?

source.notes: 60 = one full cycle of the heavenly stems × earthly branches.

questions.q: What is the most popular birthday gift item in the U.S. — also the world's largest single greeting-card category?

source.notes: audit-corrected 2026-05-09: rewrote nonsensical stem; birthdays are the #1 card-sending occasion in the U.S. — over 7 billion sold annually.

questions.q: What is the date of the U.S. president's official 'birthday holiday' celebrating Washington and Lincoln?

source.notes: Officially Washington's Birthday; commonly called Presidents' Day.

questions.q: On which date is George Washington's actual birthday?

source.notes: Washington was born February 22, 1732 (Gregorian).

questions.q: On which date is Abraham Lincoln's birthday?

source.notes: Born February 12, 1809, in a log cabin in Hardin County, Kentucky.

questions.q: How many people on Earth, on average, share your birthday?

source.notes: 8 billion people / 365 days ≈ 22 million per day.

questions.q: In a group of how many people is there a 50% chance two share a birthday (the 'Birthday Paradox')?

source.notes: Just 23 people; counterintuitively low.

questions.q: Which famous 1962 song was sung breathily by Marilyn Monroe to JFK at his 45th birthday celebration?
choices: I Wanna Be Loved by You
Diamonds Are a Girl's Best Friend

source.notes: Madison Square Garden, May 19, 1962.

questions.q: Whose 1980/1981 single 'Happy Birthday' was written to lobby for a Martin Luther King Jr. holiday?

source.notes: audit-corrected 2026-05-09: removed duplicate 'Stevie Wonder' option and 'duo' framing; Stevie Wonder's 'Happy Birthday' (Hotter than July, 1980) lobbied for the MLK Jr. holiday.

questions.q: Which Mexican-Catholic custom celebrates one's saint-day in addition to (or instead of) one's birthday?

source.notes: Honored on the feast day of the saint after which one is named.

questions.q: Why does the British monarch celebrate two birthdays each year?
choices: An actual birthday and an 'official' summer Trooping the Colour

source.notes: Official birthday is in June for better Trooping the Colour weather.

questions.q: Which famous duo Patty and Mildred Hill are credited with composing the melody of 'Happy Birthday to You'?

source.notes: Kentucky kindergarten teacher Patty Hill + her sister Mildred wrote 'Good Morning to All' in 1893.

questions.q: Which day of the year is the most common birthday in the U.S., per CDC data?

source.notes: audit-corrected 2026-05-09: per CDC 1994-2014 data, September 9 is the single most common U.S. birthday; mid-September dominates (~9 months after December holidays).
